name: Hubbard Squash-Almond Crisp
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Toast almonds and chop finely.
3. Halve the squash lengthwise and remove seeds and fiber. Place squash, cut side down, in a shallow baking dish large enough to hold the squash in a single layer. Pour in water. Bake, covered for 30 minutes.
4. Remove dish from oven and turn squash over. Cool. Use a spoon to remove the flesh. Cut into 2-inch cubes. Place squash in a bowl, drizzle with brandy, sprinkle with sugar, and set aside for 30 minutes to macerate.
5. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon. Mix in butter, working with your fingers until the mixture holds together and is crumbly. Stir in chopped almonds and crushed amaretti cookies. Set aside.
6. Place squash and juice in a 12-inch round baking dish. Spread almond mixture evenly over them. Bake for 1 hour, or until the squash is bubbling and the topping is golden brown.
7. To serve, scoop hot crisp into individual dessert bowls and drizzle with chilled heavy cream.
